articles.title: Heatwave in AP Intensifies, Records 43.7°C at Rayalapeta - Deccan Chronicle
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Andhra Pradesh
articles.full_text:
AP State Disaster Management Agency managing director Prakhar Jain advised the public to take appropriate precautions against the gradually intensifying heat, heatwaves, and sultry weather.
Severe heatwave continues to grip Andhra Pradesh, with temperatures soaring past 40°C in several districts. On Wednesday, Rayalapeta in Chittoor district recorded the highest temperature at 43.7°C, while 143 mandals across 22 districts reported extreme conditions.
AP State Disaster Management Agency managing director Prakhar Jain advised the public to take appropriate precautions against the gradually intensifying heat, heatwaves, and sultry weather.
He disclosed that maximum temperatures on Thursday are likely to range between 43°C and 44°C, with heatwaves expected in G.K. Veedhi in ASR district and in Chintur, Kunavaram, and Gurtedu of Polavaram district.
Prakhar Jain further said heatwaves will prevail in 16 mandals on Friday, with possibility of a severe heatwave.
Under the influence of a low-pressure area, scattered rains accompanied by lightning are expected in Rayalaseema districts over the next two days.
On Wednesday, maximum temperatures recorded are as follows: 42.6°C in Dornipadu (Nandyal district), 42.5°C in Nagaradona (Kurnool district), 42°C in Kalasapadu (YSR Kadapa district), 41.9°C in Kambham (Markapuram district), 41.8°C in Vuyyuru (Krishna district) and Thorragudipadu (NTR district), 41.3°C in Devarapalli (Anakapalli district), 41.2°C in Battuvanipalli (Anantapur district), Bellamkonda (Palnadu district), and Dadithota (Sri Sathya Sai district), and 41°C in Marripadu (Nellore district).
